A lock-free ring buffer manages wraparound by treating the underlying array as a circular address space, where the head and tail indices are maintained as monotonically increasing atomic counters rather than resetting to zero. This design allows the system to distinguish between full and empty states by comparing the difference between the tail and head counters against the buffer capacity, effectively avoiding the ambiguity of identical indices that plagues fixed-size circular buffers. Memory ordering is critical to ensure visibility of written data across threads; producers must use release semantics when updating the tail index to guarantee that all prior writes to the buffer slots are visible to consumers before the new tail value is published, while
